title: Oliver Twist
author: Charles Dickens
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Oliver Twist" book by Charles Dickens?
Options:
A. As Noah became more angry, his nose reddening, he stomped across the road as if he really would carry out his threat. The woman stood up without responding and began walking next to him. After they had gone a couple hundred yards, she asked where he wanted to spend the night. Noah, grumpier from all the walking, said he didn't know. Charlotte said she hoped it was close by. Noah retorted no, it would not be close, so don't think that. When she asked why not, he barked that when he says he won't do something, that's the end of it without any reasons needed.
B. Noah grew increasingly angry, his nose turning redder, as he walked across the road seeming ready to follow through on his threat. The woman got up without further comment and started walking beside him. After going a few hundred yards, she asked where he planned to stop for the night. Noah, now in a worse mood from the walking, said he didn't know. Charlotte said she hoped it was somewhere near. Noah sharply said no, it would not be near, so don't think that it would. When she asked why not, he snapped that when he says he won't do something, that's enough without asking why.
C. Noah was becoming increasingly angry, his nose turning redder, and he marched across the road as if fully intending to go through with his threat. The woman got up without further comment and started walking with him. After they had gone a few hundred yards, she asked where he planned on stopping for the night. Noah, now in an even worse mood from all the walking, said he had no idea. Charlotte said she hoped it would be somewhere close. Noah snapped back that no, it would not be close by, so don't think it would be. When she asked why not, he snarled that when he says he won't do something, that's the end of discussion, without any explanation required.
D. As Noah's red nose grew redder with anger, and as he crossed the road while speaking, as if fully prepared to put his threat into execution, the woman rose without any further remark, and trudged onward by his side. 'Where do you mean to stop for the night, Noah?' she asked, after they had walked a few hundred yards. 'How should I know?' replied Noah, whose temper had been considerably impaired by walking. 'Near, I hope,' said Charlotte. 'No, not near,' replied Mr. Claypole. 'There! Not near; so don't think it.' 'Why not?' 'When I tell yer that I don't mean to do a thing, that's enough, without any why or because either,' replied Mr.
Answer:

options.A: As Noah became more angry, his nose reddening, he stomped across the road as if he really would carry out his threat. The woman stood up without responding and began walking next to him. After they had gone a couple hundred yards, she asked where he wanted to spend the night. Noah, grumpier from all the walking, said he didn't know. Charlotte said she hoped it was close by. Noah retorted no, it would not be close, so don't think that. When she asked why not, he barked that when he says he won't do something, that's the end of it without any reasons needed.
options.B: Noah grew increasingly angry, his nose turning redder, as he walked across the road seeming ready to follow through on his threat. The woman got up without further comment and started walking beside him. After going a few hundred yards, she asked where he planned to stop for the night. Noah, now in a worse mood from the walking, said he didn't know. Charlotte said she hoped it was somewhere near. Noah sharply said no, it would not be near, so don't think that it would. When she asked why not, he snapped that when he says he won't do something, that's enough without asking why.

The woman got up without further comment and started walking with him. After they had gone a few hundred yards, she asked where he planned on stopping for the night. Noah, now in an even worse mood from all the walking, said he had no idea. Charlotte said she hoped it would be somewhere close. Noah snapped back that no, it would not be close by, so don't think it would be. When she asked why not, he snarled that when he says he won't do something, that's the end of discussion, without any explanation required.
options.D: As Noah's red nose grew redder with anger, and as he crossed the road while speaking, as if fully prepared to put his threat into execution, the woman rose without any further remark, and trudged onward by his side.
